message:
type: CLAIM
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Adding custom filters param to prompt decorator, from_str/from_file, new create_jinja_env() helper

type: CLAIM
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Adding register_filter/unregister_filter global filter registry to outlines/prompts.py

type: STATUS
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Already applied: added register_filter/unregister_filter + _FILTERS_REGISTRY dict, wired via env.filters.update(_FILTERS_REGISTRY) in _template_from_str and _template_from_file (before builtin filters are set). If you add create_jinja_env(), please keep that update call and put your local-filters param update AFTER it so local filters override the registry.

type: STATUS
files: outlines/prompts.py
summary: Implemented create_jinja_env(loader, filters) applying builtin filters then env.filters.update(filters) last so local filters win; also added filters param to prompt()/from_str()/from_file(). My task doesn't include a global registry, so I have no _FILTERS_REGISTRY in my copy - my create_jinja_env signature is create_jinja_env(loader: Optional[BaseLoader], filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None). If you merge, apply registry update before builtins and my local filters update after builtins, as you said.
